tender.title: GB-Colchester: 0349 - Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund Wave 2.1
tender.description:
Colchester City Homes on behalf of Colchester Borough Council (the Council) wished to commission a contractor to undertake Social Housing Decarbonisation, ensuring PAS2030/PAS2035-compliant retrofit of 105 Council-owned, occupied domestic properties as part of Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund Wave 2.1 funding for Colchester City Council.
      
      Bids are being sought utilising the Fusion 21 - Decarbonisation Works Framework and only companies that have previously expressed interest have been invited to tender.
      
      The tender is split into three separate package of work (35 properties per package), for which a tender may be submitted, and the Council will appoint separate contractors for each of the 3 packages.
      
      Full details of each package are contained within the tender document's.

tender.title: P1190 - MONITORING VISITS TO RESIDENTIAL CHILDREN'S HOMES AND SUPPORTED ACCOMMODATION - AWARD
tender.description: Birmingham Children's Trust is inviting you are invited to submit a quotation for Monitoring Visits to Residential Children's Homes and Supported Accommodation, no later than 12:00pm, on 5th May 2023. The Trust requires a number of suppliers for the provision of full quality assurance visits, thematic monitoring and safe and well visits to named residential homes or supported accommodation providers on a call off basis, in urgent situations (24-hour response time).  Birmingham Children's Trust will provide any pre visit intelligence and a brief about the purpose of the visit and the provider is to produce a report thereon.

tender.title: Supported Accommodation for Young single people aged 16 - 25 (Doncaster Foyer)
tender.description:
Doncaster Borough Council invites tenders for experienced providers to provide a support service at Doncaster Foyer for 16 to 25 years olds.

Doncaster Foyer forms part of Doncaster's Resettlement Pathway for people experiencing (or at risk of) homelessness including those with complex needs.

The accommodation supplied is a 40 unit purpose built Foyer style accommodation facility in Doncaster City centre. The Registered Housing Provider of the accommodation is Salvation	Army Housing Association.  

There may be Individuals placed within the Service via Doncaster's Childrens social care teams and Early Help.  Referrals from social care will receive the same support, access and opportunities within Doncaster Foyer as those placed by the Single Point of Access (SPOA).  There will be no distinction in the support provided.  The Council will cover the accommodation related costs such as rent and Service charge if Housing Benefit / Universal Credit is not applicable.

The Provider will offer a safe, supportive environment and be committed to providing a 	sensitive, non-judgemental and non-oppressive support Service. 
The Provider will 	need to 	clearly demonstrate a commitment to increasing the Individual's 	academic achievement, 	training and employment opportunities and support Individual's to make a successful transition to sustainable independent living.

The Provider will be an accredited member of the Foyer Federation or achieve full accreditation within 12 months of Services Commencement Date. See link for further detail:   https://www.foyer.net/pages/accreditation

It is anticipated that the contract will commence on 01 April 2023 and run until 31/03/2026.   There is also an option to extend the contract by up to further 2 X 12 months at the sole discretion of the council.

Additional information: Contract value includes initial 3 year period and 2 x 12 month extensions

tender.title: Move On Accommodation Service
tender.description:
The aim of the contract is to provide high quality Move On (Second Stage - Step Down) accommodation for homeless people who need a short period of support prior to rehousing into a range of community settings.  The accommodation will be aimed at people requiring low level support who have already completed a comprehensive support programme and are ready to move on to more independent accommodation.  It will be primarily aimed at those who have previously been placed in council commissioned Temporary Accommodation services or those placed into emergency hotel provision by the council. The service will be for those with low level support needs and will be provided on a temporary basis prior to a move into the community. \r
\r
Objectives of the contract: the objectives of the contract are as follows:\r
\r
a)	To further prepare service users to move to independent accommodation in the community.\r
\r
b)	To increase the independence of service users and therefore levels of planned move on through the provision of a high-quality Move On accommodation and support service\r
\r
c)	To provide on-site support to increase and further develop long term independent living skills, reducing the cycle of repeat homelessness \r
\r
d)	To minimise the length of stay in the Move On service and achieve timely move on\r
\r
e)	To contribute to the shorter lengths of stay in wider temporary accommodation services commissioned by the council and a reduction in the use of emergency hotels for single people at risk of homelessness, by providing a suitable, supported alternative for people with low level needs\r
\r
f)	To ensure that the holistic needs and risks of service users are fully assessed and support plans addressing move on are put in place\r
\r
g)	To assist service users to access primary care & secondary Health provision, and Mental Health services, and any other service which meets their needs\r
\r
h)	To work with service users to increase their recovery options by identifying open, accessible and targeted drug and alcohol services available to all service users and ensuring they can be supported if they drop out of treatment, to reengage with appropriate provision\r
\r
i)	To offer opportunities for training, digital and social inclusion, meaningful occupation and to help individuals to prepare for employment\r
\r
j)	To deliver a non-judgemental and inclusive services which treats service users with dignity, respecting gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental health ability, religion, culture, social background and lifestyle choice\r
\r
k)	To deliver added social value that will provide benefits to the local community and contribute to the economic, social and environmental wellbeing of Liverpool\r
\r
l)	To deliver efficiencies and offer best value for Liverpool City Council.\r

tender.title: Decarbonisation of Social Housing Scheme (Works)
tender.description: The Authority applied for a grant for Energy Efficiency Funding, Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund Wave 2 and Procurement of Energy Efficiency Retrofit Works.  In anticipation of being successfully awarded the grant, an assessment of all suppliers within Lot 16 (Whole House Internal & External Installation Works more than £1m) of the EN:Procure Installation & Repairs Framework were assessed by EN:Procure with EQUANS Services Limited demonstrating best value for money overall, they have therefore been awarded a direct award.
